calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Confirm Automatic Stay Pending Appeal Or, In The Alternative, For Discretionary Stay Pending Appeal
Off Calendar Aug-28-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s Motion To Confirm Automatic Stay Pending Appeal Or, In The Alternative, For Discretionary Stay Pending Appeal.

Respondent Uber Technologies, Inc.'s motion to confirm automatic stay pending appeal or, in the alternative, for discretionary stay pending appeal is granted. This case is stayed in this court until the conclusion of the pending appeal or a ruling by this court or the Court of Appeal to the contrary. Uber's appeal resulted in a stay on all proceedings in this court per CCP 916(a). Even if it did not, this court exercises its discretion per CCP 918(a) to stay all proceedings in this court in order to preserve the jurisdiction of the Court of Appeal and to further judicial economy.

Order signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/HK)

MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Seal Portions Of The Declaration Of Greg Kato
Off Calendar Jun-22-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Owen J. Clements, Deputy City Attorney (415-554-3944) Chief of Special Litigation of City and County of San Francisco for PETITIONERS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO TREASURER JOSE CISNEROS and SAN FRANCISCO TAX COLLECTOR DAVID AUGUSTINE
Allison Davis, Esq. (415-276-6580) and Sanjay Nangia, Esq. (415-276-6577) of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP for Respondent UBER TECHNOLOGIE INC.

RULING - Respondent reports the matter is unopposed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION To Seal Portions Of The Declaration Of Greg Kato is GRANTED. No opposition filed and good cause shown per CRC 2.550(d). Order is signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/RBU)

Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of San Franciscos Motion For An Order To Require Uber Technologies, Inc. To Disclose Driver Contact Information To The San Francisco Tax Collector
Off Calendar Jun-22-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Owen J. Clements, Deputy City Attorney (415-554-3944) Chief of Special Litigation of City and County of San Francisco for PETITIONERS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO TREASURER JOSE CISNEROS and SAN FRANCISCO TAX COLLECTOR DAVID AUGUSTINE
Allison Davis, Esq. (415-276-6580) and Sanjay Nangia, Esq. (415-276-6577) of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP for Respondent UBER TECHNOLOGIE INC.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court informs parties of it's questions. Petitioner and Respondent responds to the Court's inquiries. The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modification as follows:

PETITIONERS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO TREASURER JOSE CISNEROS and SAN FRANCISCO TAX COLLECTOR DAVID AUGUSTINE'S Motion For An Order To Require Uber Technologies, Inc. To Disclose Driver Contact Information To The San Francisco Tax Collector is GRANTED. Petitioners have shown that the Tax Collector is authorized by section 6.4-1 of the San Francisco Business and Tax Regulations Code to issue the administrative subpoena, the subpoena serves a valid purpose of investigating compliance with section 6.2-12 of the Code, and the information subpoenaed is pertinent to such a determination. Uber's quasi-constitutional challenges to the subpoena based on vagueness of the regulatory scheme and the prohibition of extraterritorial application of local ordinances are premature in light of Western Oil & Gas Assn. v. State Board of Equalization (1987) 44 Cal.3d 208, 214. Western Oil directs the court to only examine the Tax Collector's authority to issue the subpoena and not to determine the ultimate validity of the tax assessment. The subpoena is not overly broad and Uber has not shown that compliance would be unduly burdensome. Uber also failed to show that the driver information requested constitutes a protected trade secret or that disclosure would violate drivers' privacy or due process rights. Counsel to meet and confer regarding a compliance date and report back to the Court in writing on or before July 3, 2017. Order is signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/RBU)
